postData.contentHtml:
Former President Donald Trump has been making headlines with his increasingly frequent and often peculiar use of AI-generated content on his Truth Social platform. These posts have ranged from a video depicting him humorously tossing Stephen Colbert into a dumpster and dancing, to sharing AI-generated images of aliens and futuristic command centers.

This surge in AI-assisted social media activity has not gone unnoticed. Critics have described his posts as "nonsensical" and "bizarre," leading to a flurry of speculation and commentary. One particularly pointed moment occurred when a "Trump Nemesis" reportedly "roasted" him over an AI post with a "wedding dig." While specific details are scarce due to reporting challenges, it appears some of these controversial posts coincided with the morning of his eldest son's wedding, providing ample fodder for critics to weigh in.

The public's reaction to Trump's AI posts appears to be mixed. Simulated social sentiment indicates a spectrum of opinions, ranging from "mostly positive" views to significant "debate" and critical engagement. This suggests that while some may view his creative use of AI with amusement or indifference, many others are actively questioning the motivations and implications behind his digital output. As AI technology becomes more accessible, its integration into political discourse, even in the form of lighthearted or provocative content, continues to evolve and challenge traditional communication norms.
postData.title: Trump's AI Adventures on Truth Social: From Colbert to Aliens
postData.summary: Former President Donald Trump's recent foray into AI-generated content on Truth Social has sparked widespread reactions, from political roasting to online confusion.

sources.title: Trump Nemesis Roasts Him Over AI Post With Wedding Dig
Trump unravels in alarming Truth Social rampage
'Target destroyed' Trump shares set of AI-generated alien posts
Donald Trump’s latest AI image share sparks confusion
